Football.title: Liverpool handed Champions League boost with Galatasaray fans BANNED from Anfield for second leg
Football.content:
LIVERPOOL have been handed a major Champions League last 16 boost with all Galatasaray fans banned from the second leg at Anfield.

Arne Slot‘s side travel to Turkey next week for a potentially intimidating first leg tie in Istanbul’s RAMS Park.





But while the 54,000-capacity ground in Turkey‘s biggest city will be a bear pit for the Reds, there will be no away fans in Liverpool for the second leg.

It is the punishment imposed by UEFA after Galatasaray supporters threw missiles, lit flares and engaged in other rowdy behaviour as they eliminated Juventus in extra time last week

The Turkish side initially threw away a 5-2 first leg advantage but two goals in extra time sealed their place in the next round.

Euro chiefs, though, announced: “The Uefa Appeals Board has decided to fine Galatasaray £34,700 and to ban Galatasaray from selling tickets to its away supporters for the next Uefa club competition match for throwing of objects, lighting of fireworks and crowd disturbances.”



That appears to be the end of the process with no further right of appeal from the Turkish club, meaning Anfield will be a Galatasaray fan-free zone on March 18.

In the Premier League, Arne Slot’s side suffered a damaging blow in their bid to defend their crown following a 2–1 defeat to bottom-placed Wolves.

They have endured a topsy-turvy season, starting strongly in the league before going through a period where they simply could not win games.

They appeared to have found their feet again in recent weeks, but the defeat to Wolves has brought them crashing back down.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

Injuries have not helped, with big-money signing Alexandre Isak not having played since December 22.

The Swedish striker was expected to provide the goals needed to defend the title, but it has been a tough start to life at Anfield.

German star Florian Wirtz also missed the game against Wolves due to a back problem, which was another huge blow for the team.

As it stands, the Reds will hope to excel in the Champions League to make up for their Premier League struggles.

Football.title: How Chelsea became a better team WITHOUT Cole Palmer after England star’s injury woes
Football.content:
CHELSEA are a better team WITHOUT Cole Palmer.

If someone told you that in 2024, you’d have called them crazy, and you would have been right to do so.





In 2024, Cole Palmer WAS Chelsea.

So much so that Mauricio Pochettino challenged the Blues players to prove they were not ‘Cole Palmer FC’, before being beaten 5-0 by Arsenal…

But football moves fast, and in 2026, that is a fact.

In April 2024, ahead of that game against the Gunners, Chelsea‘s win percentage was 43.5% with him starting in the side, and dropped to 37.5% in the eight games he hadn’t started.



Palmer’s goals alone earned Chelsea a massive 13 points at that moment, the third-biggest individual contribution in the Premier League.

Chelsea have won 73% of their games this season without Palmer, compared with just 24% with him.

Palmer, of course, is a goalscorer for the Blues, and someone the team looks to when they need to put the ball in the back of the net, but this season, penalties have been masking some pretty poor showings in front of goal.

So much so that they have improved from 1.5 to 2.2 goals per game in his absence.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

IS THAT GOOD NEWS?

In one sense, yes.

Two seasons ago, the Blues were SO overly reliant on their ice-cold attacking star that they looked a shadow of themselves without him.

Opponents could game-plan specifically around how to stop Palmer, because if you stopped him, you stopped Chelsea.

Now, the Blues are a more frightening prospect overall.



The emergence of Estevao has been vital to that, with the Brazilian wonderkid really stepping up in Palmer’s absence, while the signing of Joao Pedro has added significant threat to Liam Rosenior‘s front line.

Without Palmer, Enzo Fernandez was another player who seemed to thrive in a box-crashing midfield role, as opposed to a deeper playmaking one.

BUT… BUT… PALMER IS THEIR BEST PLAYER?



Also correct. In fact, these two truths can co-exist.

It is no secret that Palmer is a calibre of player incredibly hard to come by.

He finished eighth in the Ballon d’Or rankings and is still regarded within Chelsea as “untouchable”, despite links with a move to Manchester United in the summer.

This season, though, it looks as though he gets brain freeze unless he’s stood over the ball from the penalty spot.

After lighting up the Premier League for two successive seasons, he’s been in the clutches of decline this campaign.



Palmer has seven goals and three assists in 16 matches since returning from a groin issue, that has plagued him this season, in December.

Four of those goals, though, have been from the penalty spot.

Enzo Maresca couldn’t get the best out of Palmer in the same way that Mauricio Pochettino did, and one of Rosenior’s biggest tasks will be figuring out how to re-ignite the ice-cold enigma of the 23/24 season.

SunSport’s Lloyd Canfield delved into how Rosenior can get Palmer back to his best this season, here.



The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube Calling all Chelsea fans - we have a brand new YouTube channel! The Sun’s Chelsea YouTube channel will be your go-to destination for the latest news, transfer updates, match previews, post-match analysis, exclusive interviews, fan reactions, and breaking stories from Stamford Bridge. Subscribe to The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube now

THEN WHAT IS THE ANSWER?

The scary-good version of Chelsea is a functioning system, with a mentally and physically fit Cole Palmer at the core of it.

A player with his level of ability is too rare not to persist with, but the Blues need to get the best out of him sooner rather than later, or he will risk being remembered in the same vein asDele Alli at Spurs – one electric season, followed by a few decent ones before a subsequent drop-off.

When Rosenior gets that recipe right, is when you will see Chelsea compete at the highest level once more.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The Club World Cup proved it, the Blues had Palmer firing, and with the addition of Joao Pedro, they swept UCL last-16 opponents PSG under the rug with ease.



Their visit to Paris next week will draw stark comparisons to that summer finale, with the Blues needing to progress if they want to win European silverware once again this season.

Football.title: Fenerbahce manager Domenico Tedesco rushed to hospital and undergoing treatment for ‘severe infection’
Football.content:
MANAGER SHOCK Fenerbahce manager Domenico Tedesco rushed to hospital and undergoing treatment for ‘severe infection’

FENERBAHCE head coach Domenico Tedesco has been rushed to hospital.

The 40-year-old is undergoing treatment for a severe infection, with a club statement reading: “Our Technical Director Domenico Tedesco’s treatment process is ongoing due to the severe infection he has contracted.





“Accordingly, our coach will not be able to lead our team in today’s Ziraat Turkish Cup Group C Week 4 match against Gaziantep FK.

“In the aforementioned match, our assistant coach Zeki Murat Göle will assume technical responsibility for our team.

“It is planned that our Technical Director Domenico Tedesco will lead our team in the Samsunspor match we will play at the weekend.”

Tedesco was unable to manage Fenerbahce in their match against Antalyaspor on Sunday due to illness.



The Turkish giants went on to draw the game 2-2 at the Antalya Stadium.

A statement ahead of the clash read: “Our Technical Director Domenico Tedesco has been placed under observation due to a viral infection and high fever.

“Due to his illness, he will not be able to lead our team in tonight’s match against H. Antalyaspor.

“We extend our wishes for a speedy recovery to our Technical Director Domenico Tedesco.”



The head coach’s assistant, Zeki Murat Göle, will take charge of Fenerbahce’s Turkish Cup match against Gaziantep on Wednesday evening.

Tedesco is currently expected to return to the dugout for the club’s upcoming Super Lig match against Samsunspor on Sunday.

The club will likely provide another update on his condition at the weekend.

Fenerbahce currently sit second in the league table, four points behind Galatasaray.

Football.title: Darren Fletcher’s son Jack’s grovelling apology in full as Man Utd starlet slapped with ban for homophobic slur
Football.content:
COMING CLEAN Darren Fletcher’s son Jack’s grovelling apology in full as Man Utd starlet slapped with ban for homophobic slur

MANCHESTER UNITED star Jack Fletcher has issued an apology after he was banned by the FA over a homophobic slur.

Fletcher, 18, who is the son of United coach Darren, has been given a six-match suspension, a £1,500 fine and education order after an incident during a United Under-21s game.





The youngster has accepted the charge after using language “involving a reference to sexual orientation” during a 5-2 EFL trophy defeat to Barnsley in October.

SunSport understands Fletcher was abused throughout the game about his family by a rival player.

Fletcher snapped and is said to have asked why his opponent was so aware of his background and whether he was a “gay boy”.

He has now apologised for his words and said they did not “reflect my beliefs or values”.



The teenager said: “I am truly sorry for the offensive word that I used in the heat of the moment.

“Despite the fact that I had no intention to use the term as a homophobic insult, I completely understand that such language is unacceptable and immediately apologised after the game.

“I want to be clear that this momentary lapse of character absolutely does not reflect my beliefs or values.”

United added on their official website: “Manchester United has worked with Jack to strengthen his understanding of discriminatory language, and why it is harmful.



“In addition to his ongoing participation in regular Academy programmes on diversity and inclusion, Jack will also take part in educational training through the FA.

“Manchester United prides itself on being an inclusive and welcoming club.”

Fletcher had started the Under-21s game in midfield and opened the scoring in the seventh minute.

But he was shown a straight red card by the referee after his choice of words was reported.



The full FA statement read: “It is alleged that during the fixture, [Fletcher] acted in an improper manner and/or used abusive and/or insulting words, contrary to FA Rule E3.1.

“It is alleged that the breach of FA Rule E3.1 is an ‘Aggravated Breach’, as defined in FA Rule E3.2, as it included a reference, whether express or implied, to sexual orientation.

“Jack Fletcher admitted the charge and elected a paper hearing.”

Fletcher has been heavily involved with the first team this season and has made three senior appearances in the 2025-26 campaign.

The player’s twin brother Tyler also plays for United after both joined the club from Man City in 2023.

Football.title: EFL owner, 76, braced to sell club after suffering nasty injury – just months after saving them from extinction
Football.content:
READING could be on the lookout for a new OWNER.

Reading FC and chairman Rob Couhig, 76, has admitted he may walk away from the club as he is actively entertaining offers.



The Royals chief was forced into a hip replacement earlier this year after a New Year’s Eve mishap left him needing surgery.



And he said the “little setback” delivered a “sharp” reminder he can’t keep doing 20-hour flights to Berkshire forever.

But it’s not as if him and co-owner Todd Trosclair – who ended the reign of Dai Yongge’s controversial ownership in May 2025 – haven’t been putting in the cash.

After a hectic January window, Couhig revealed to the Reading Chronicle that they have already been plugging a £1 million-a-month hole to keep Reading on track.

Reading have faced huge financial hurdles from transfer embargoes, spending restrictions and even a winding-up petition from HMRC in late 2025.



But the Royals have 12 games left of the season – and they’re two points away from the play-off spots in eighth.

And Couhig’s phone has been ringing off the hook from interested buyers and investors.

A new long-term partnership with cybersecurity company Barracuda has brought hope in the last few weeks too.

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

Couhig – former owner of Wycombe Wanderers – has put it down to the club’s current position and that it has become an asset.



When pitched by the Chronicle whether he would rather keep or sell, he said: “Both. I want to own the club because I enjoy it.”

And after the chaos, the Royals are finally in their strongest financial shape for years – helped by new investment, commercial deals and results on the pitch.

Couhig added: “The days I don’t want to own the club is when I’m getting off the plane, having flown for 20 hours and I’m thinking what can I do?

“The little setback with my hip brought in the sharp reality of the fact that I’m 76 years old.

“Am I going to want to commute here for the next 10 years? Probably not.



“Am I willing to do it for the next five years where we bring this club to where we can be? Probably.

“On the other hand, you know, some guy knocks at your door, what are you supposed to say?”

Football.title: Premier League club’s £50m stadium expansion plans are confirmed – with capacity set to DOUBLE
Football.content:
A PREMIER League football club’s stadium is set to double in capacity after revamp plans costing an eye-watering sum were approved.

AFC Bournemouth will splash around £50m to redevelop the Vitality Stadium as part of ambitious expansion plans.





The Daily Echo reports that the club paid £10million to buy back their Dean Court home last year from property company Structadene, with the figure revealed in a Black Knight Football Club document.

The same presentation states the Cherries are “currently in the process of redeveloping and expanding” the ground at an estimated cost of around £50m.

The overhaul comes after enabling works were approved by Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole Council (BCP), with work already getting underway.

Plans will see the current 11,000-capacity stadium dramatically expanded, effectively doubling its size to more than 20,000.

Currently the stadium is the smallest in the Premier League.

The document, published by Cannae Holdings, Inc, also sheds light on the club’s rapid rise since Black Knight Football Club took over.

It reads: “In December 2022, BKFC purchased 100% of AFCB for £120M, marking the first acquisition in BKFC’s multi-club strategy.”

The presentation adds that shortly after acquiring the club, owners began developing a new training centre costing around £42m, which opened in April 2025.



“That same month, BKFC acquired AFCB’s stadium for £10M and is currently in the process of redeveloping and expanding AFCB’s stadium for an estimated £50M,” the document states.

Chairman Bill Foley previously confirmed the deal to buy back the stadium at the opening of the club’s new performance centre last year.

Financially, the Cherries are also booming.

Total revenue has risen around 40 per cent from £143m in 2022/23 to £199m in 2024/25.

Ticket revenue is up more than 27 per cent and season ticket renewals for 2025/26 topped 98 per cent — figures owners expect to climb even higher once the revamped stadium opens its doors.

Football.title: Arne Slot warned he will '100 percent' be sacked if Liverpool don't reach Champions League
Football.content:
Arne Slot warned he will '100 percent' be sacked if Liverpool don't reach Champions League

Liverpool were dealt a damaging loss on Tuesday night as they were beaten by Wolves with Arne Slot warned Champions League failure could cost him his job at Anfield

View 2 Images Arne Slot could face a battle to retain his job if Liverpool miss out on the Champions League (Image: Carl Recine/Getty Images)

Liverpool boss Arne Slot has been told he will "100 percent be sacked" if he fails to lead the team into the Champions League.

‌



The Reds lost for the ninth time in the Premier League on Tuesday night as bottom side Wolves scored in stoppage time to secure a memorable win. It leaves the Merseysiders in fifth, but Chelsea can go above them with a win on Wednesday.

‌



Liverpool began the season with five wins from five and looked a good bet to retain their Premier League title. However they've fallen off a cliff since then with their campaign marred by inconsistencies despite a summer spend that exceeded £400million and twice saw them break the British transfer record.

‌



Slot was dealing with hefty expectations but the defending champions will not only relinquish their crown, they could also miss out on Europe's elite competition and the riches that come with it.

Jamie Carragher has previously claimed the Dutchman "can't have a leg to stand on" if he misses out on the top five and now Jamie O'Hara is adamant he'll face the chop if he finishes sixth or lower.

Jurgen Klopp managerial return stance changing despite RB chief issuing feisty response READ MORE :

Trent Alexander-Arnold gets thumbs up to complete Liverpool return amid Anfield issues READ MORE :

The ex-Tottenham midfielder insists last season's success counts for nothing as he said on Sky Sports: "He's in big trouble Arne Slot, he needs to get some results, he needs to get Champions League football. If he doesn't get Champions League he'll be sacked, 100 percent. You can't keep Arne Slot just because he won the Premier League last season.

‌



"You have to have Champions League football, they spent a lot of money, got top players. We're talking about five teams being in the Champions League and Liverpool aren't one of them, Arne Slot can't be the manager."

View 2 Images The Reds have dropped off massively given their title success last term

Liverpool have won just four of the 11 league games they've played since the turn of the year and O'Hara doesn't see huge scope for improvement in this team - suggesting this is now their level.

Article continues below

"I don't see how he's going to turn it around, that's my concern," he claimed. "I don't see a team thats all of a sudden going to start playing really good football again. Can they be the team they were last season, where they were hard to beat? I wouldn't say they were breathtaking to watch, but they were a good side in a poor league, but I don't see an Arne Slot team getting better than what they are right now."

Football.title: Daniel Farke's Leeds future in limbo as Elland Road chiefs postpone new contract talks
Football.content:
Daniel Farke's Leeds future in limbo as Elland Road chiefs postpone new contract talks

Daniel Farke will have to keep Leeds in the top flight if he wants to negotiate a new contract at Elland Road following Tuesday's home defeat by Sunderland

View Image Daniel Farke's future has been put on hold at Leeds

Leeds United have postponed contract talks with Daniel Farke until the end of the season. And Farke might have to keep Leeds in the Premier League in order to secure a new deal at Elland Road.

‌



The German will enter the final 12 months of his current deal in June. He is keen to remain in charge of Leeds, whatever happens in the coming weeks, and had been hoping to have held negations before now.

‌



But talks have been put on hold - while club bosses wait to see if he can keep Leeds in the Premier League. Leeds are six pints above the drop zone with nine games remaining.

‌



But Farke is coming under renewed pressure following a dismal run of just two wins in 13 league games.

This includes Tuesday's dismal home loss to Sunderland, which saw Farke's men blow a golden chance to take a huge step towards survival.

‌



Leeds manager Daniel Farke prepared to make U-turn on controversial Man City red card READ MORE :

Arsenal know two results this weekend could mean Premier League title almost in the bag READ MORE :

Leeds chairman Paraag Marathe and major investor Pete Lowy could be seen in deep conversation in Dakota Hotel in the wake of their side's crushing home defeat to Sunderland.

And Lowy has made it clear Farke's immediate future will not be decided until the outcome of the season is known.

He said: "We have not yet stayed in the Premier League. We are not yet secured, and so we're not talking about it. To talk about Daniel's contract now is just not on the table. Not for him and not for us. We have one goal."

Article continues below

Farke took charge of Leeds in 2023 on a deal until 2027. He led them to promotion to the top flight in his second season in charge, winning the Championship title on goal difference with 100 points.

Football.title: Ex-Liverpool star scolds Mo Salah for 'horrible' moment in embarrassing Wolves defeat
Football.content:
Ex-Liverpool star scolds Mo Salah for 'horrible' moment in embarrassing Wolves defeat

Liverpool suffered a shock 2-1 defeat to Wolves on Tuesday to dent their Champions League hopes, and despite scoring, Mo Salah has been slated for his performance

View 2 Images Mo Salah's goal didn't mask his overall performance (Image: Dan Istitene/Getty Images)

Former Liverpool star Steve Nicol has picked out a 'horrible' decision from Mo Salah which summed up the Reds' embarrassing 2-1 loss away to Wolves. Liverpool were stunned by the Premier League's worst team, who before Tuesday had only won two home games all season and remain rock bottom of the table.

‌



Salah, 33, did equalise for Arne Slot's side shortly after Rodrigo Gomes had given Wolves the lead in the 78th minute. But Liverpool couldn't even hold on to a draw, as Andre Gomes' stoppage-time strike deflected off Joe Gomez and nestled in the back of the net.

‌



Wolves were clinical, scoring with half of their four attempts. In contrast, Liverpool fired off 19 shots, with Salah's goal coming rather fortuitously, as Wolves goalkepeer Jose Sa made an error.

‌



Nicol, who enjoyed a trophy-laden 13 years at Anfield, took aim at the Egyptian's lack of involvement and highlighted one particularly poor decision which could've turned the tide at Molineux. Speaking on ESPN, the former Liverpool defender explained: "In the final third, the decision was just horrible.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Jurgen Klopp managerial return stance changing despite RB chief issuing feisty response READ MORE :

Trent Alexander-Arnold gets thumbs up to complete Liverpool return amid Anfield issues READ MORE :

"The one we showed in the highlight with Mo Salah... although he scored the goal, the goalie should've saved it and he really didn't contribute anything again all evening.

‌



"I could sit here for 10 minutes and say everything that has gone wrong but [Tuesday] was a microcosm of what's gone wrong all season; poor decision-making, the front three done not much at all and defensively, you concede two goals from two shots."

Salah, 33, had gone 10 Premier League games without scoring a goal for the first time in his career, having blanked in Liverpool's previous game, a 5-2 victory over West Ham on Saturday. His woes have coincided with the Reds' decline, as they bid to qualify for the Champions League next season.

Nicol added: "My biggest concern is that it doesn't matter who Liverpool play against... they can lose.

‌



Should Arne Slot drop Mo Salah? Have your say in the comments section.

View 2 Images Arne Slot agreed with Steve Nicol (Image: Catherine Ivill - AMA/Getty Images)

"It's one thing to talk about big games in a season but when you're team is stepping on a field regardless of the situation and you're not sure what you're going to get, that's not a decision acceptable at a club like Liverpool. Everybody who plays against Liverpool know they're going to get a shot and probably going to score."

‌



Slot echoed Nicol's complaints about Liverpool's wasteful attack. "Same old story," the Reds boss, whose side could drop to sixth on Wednesday, bemoaned.

"Recently, we are picking up points because many times we've scored from set pieces, but what did not change in the last five, six or seven games is that we struggle and find it very hard to score from the open-play chances that we do create."

Football.title: Arne Slot is being very harshly judged but the figure of Xabi Alonso is looming larger
Football.content:
Arne Slot is being very harshly judged but the figure of Xabi Alonso is looming larger

There are plenty of mitigating circumstances for Liverpool's disappointing Premier League title defence but the pressure is mounting on the Dutch manager after the defeat at Wolves

View 4 Images Arne Slot cut a forlorn figure after Liverpool's ninth Premier League defeat of the season

Common footballing consensus is that Arne Slot suffers extra critical scrutiny because he is not Jurgen Klopp. Which is a touch ironic because extra critical scrutiny shows that Slot is currently doing a little better in a season when defending a Premier League title than Klopp did in 2020/21.

‌



After winning his one and only Premier League in 19/20, Klopp had Liverpool on 46 points after 29 matches of the following campaign. After 29 matches of this campaign, Slot has Liverpool on 48 points.

‌



At this point in the 20/21 season, Klopp’s Liverpool were out of the FA Cup while Slot’s Liverpool are looking forward to a fifth round tie at Molineux on Friday night. Klopp’s Liverpool of 20/21 beat RB Leipzig in the last 16 of the Champions League but went out to Real Madrid at the quarter-final stage.

‌



Virgil van Dijk points finger after Liverpool fall to embarrassing Wolves defeat READ MORE :

Steven Gerrard singles out Liverpool star who needs to be dropped - with replacement clear READ MORE :

Next week, Slot’s Liverpool go to Istanbul for the first leg of their last-16 tie against Galatasaray. In the Premier League, Klopp’s team rallied to finish with five wins in their last five games of 20/21 but their final tally of 69 points was not great.

As always, there were mitigating circumstances. Klopp had to deal with a troublesome injury list and the Covid-based challenge of playing behind closed doors.

But there are also mitigating circumstances for Slot, who has had an equally troublesome injury list to deal with. He has also had to comfort and rally a squad that was deeply affected by the tragic passing of Diogo Jota.

‌



View 4 Images Xabi Alonso was dismissed as Real Madrid manager in early January (Image: Alvaro Medranda/Quality Sport Images/Getty Images)

The late Premier League surge meant Klopp’s class of 20/21 finished third and qualified for the Champions League but even if they had not, there was never any suggestion the German would be sacked. There are plenty suggesting Slot will be sacked.

If Liverpool do not win the Champions League and finish outside the qualification spots - not in the Premier League’s top five - then many could understand a boardroom decision to thank Slot for his sterling efforts and make a change. But it would still be very harsh.

‌



View 4 Images Jurgen Klopp had a difficult season after winning the Premier League in the 2019/20 season (Image: Getty Images)

There are, however, two words that cannot be avoided when looking at Slot’s future. Xabi Alonso. These are very early days but a couple of months since his own harsh dismissal from Real Madrid, Alonso has not been seriously linked with any future job.

More significantly, there has been no movement on Slot’s contract situation. Winning a Premier League title in the first year of your three-year deal might normally earn a manager a new contract but that did not materialise last summer.

‌



View 4 Images Arne Slot won the Premier League in his first season with Liverpool (Image: Carl Recine, Getty Images)

And it is extremely unlikely to materialise between now and the end of the season. With only a year of his contract remaining - and with Alonso available as a free agent - it would be a relatively inexpensive managerial change for Liverpool’s owners.

After his outstanding first season - and taking into account the issues he has had to deal with - it would be a managerial change that would be incredibly harsh on Slot, who has what it takes to lead a great club for a long, long time. But it is one that is becoming increasingly easy to envisage.

Football.title: Anthony Gordon to Arsenal transfer 'makes sense' as Mikel Arteta sent clear verdict
Football.content:
Anthony Gordon to Arsenal transfer 'makes sense' as Mikel Arteta sent clear verdict

Former Chelsea star Joe Cole believes Newcastle United forward Anthony Gordon would be the perfect fit for Arsenal

View 2 Images Anthony Gordon of Newcastle United has been linked with Arsenal (Image: Serena Taylor/Newcastle United via Getty Images)

Joe Cole has suggested that it 'would make sense' for Arsenal to sign Anthony Gordon this summer, despite Mikel Arteta already having two players vying for his position.

‌



The Gunners are currently battling to win their first Premier League title since the 2003/04 season, and they're also still in contention for the Carabao Cup, FA Cup and Champions League.

‌



Despite not struggling for goals this season, former England midfielder Cole believes that adding Newcastle United star Gordon to their ranks would elevate them to another level.

‌



Cole was asked about the possibility of Gordon moving to Arsenal, and pointed out that the Gunners already have two players who perform in the same position as the England international.

Declan Rice injury update drops as Mikel Arteta reveals Arsenal latest after scare READ MORE :

"I can see him fitting in there. He ticks the box of physicality for Arsenal. His mentality and physicality are top, plus he’s a winner," Cole said in an interview with Paddy Power.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"You've already got two very good players competing for that spot in [Gabriel] Martinelli and [Leandro] Trossard, but none of them tick the boxes [Anthony] Gordon does.

"Martinelli has a little bit of that physicality and mentality, and Trossard does score goals. Anthony Gordon would just give Mikel Arteta something different on that left side. But again, I just think it's a case of another huge physical body coming into that squad. He's a great player and it would make sense to me."

Former Everton striker Gordon moved to Newcastle in 2023 and has clocked up 144 appearances for the Magpies, netting 36 goals.

‌



View 2 Images Anthony Gordon has enjoyed an impressive season with Newcastle United (Image: Stu Forster/Getty Images)

The England international nearly signed for his childhood club Liverpool before the start of the 2024/25 season as Newcastle grappled with their Profitability and Sustainability accounts, but the transfer never materialised.

The 25-year-old has scored 10 goals so far this season, but just three of those have been in the Premier League as Newcastle have found themselves in 13th place in the current standings.

‌



Martinelli has managed just a single Premier League goal in 23 appearances this season, while Trossard has five to his name from 25 league outings.

Arsenal's leading Premier League scorer this season is summer signing Viktor Gyokeres, with 10 goals.

Football.title: Mikel Arteta tells fans to stop watching Premier League if they want to be entertained
Football.content:
Mikel Arteta tells fans to stop watching Premier League if they want to be entertained

Mikel Arteta has conceded that the Premier League is not as entertaining as other leagues in Europe - but that won't change unless tweaks are made to the rules

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta has called for changes in the Premier League wants to see greater entertainment (Image: Getty Images)

Mikel Arteta admits that any fan wanting to watch entertaining football needs to tune into a different country - and the Premier League needs to change its rules if it wants to bring back eye-catching games.

‌



The Arsenal boss has had to field criticism of his team's methods, notably their reliance on set pieces. They have proved hugely effective and the win over Chelsea on Sunday saw the north Londoners take their total of goals from dead-ball situations this season to 16.

‌



It has helped them establish a five-point lead at the top of the table, but the optics have divided opinion with some claiming the Premier League is less entertaining as a result of the rise in set-piece importance.

‌



Liverpool boss Arne Slot has said other European leagues offer greater entertainment value than England's top flight - putting that down in part of the use of set pieces - and Arteta admits he agrees, but that won't change unless rules are tweaked.

The Arsenal boss said: “I would like to play with three extra players in my half to get some beautiful football and always play against a free man. You want to watch that football, you have to go to a different country because in the Premier League, for the last two or three seasons, this is not the case.

Pep Guardiola makes Arsenal claim after Liverpool boss Arne Slot bemoans set-piece trend READ MORE :

“It’s going to be a different game unless we change the rules because the evolution of the game is that. Four years ago it was a completely different game.”

‌



Arteta stopped short of claiming which rules need to be altered if the Premier League is to ramp up its entertainment value. The Arsenal boss admits teams have been able to halt their opponents in open play, partly through the rise of man-to-man, rather than zonal, defending. Conversely Everton boss David Moyes wants referees to take a harder line on the grabbing and wrestling that has become common in the penalty area.

View 2 Images A reliance on set pieces has less to criticism from observers (Image: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

Arsenal are trying to outlast Manchester City in the title race and Pep Guardiola believes the Gunners won't be seen as lesser champions just because their style doesn't please everyone.

‌



"I'm pretty sure the manager is happy and the players are so happy," he said. "And I'm pretty sure you ask all the Arsenal fans and they will say 'don't change one second our set pieces. They have the feeling when it goes to set pieces, wow, I'm going to score. I'm pretty sure all the teams will want to create that vibe.

"So, enjoyable or not? Maybe, I'm not [in agreement] with the people saying 'no, that the league is not good this season, the winner will not be good'. I don't agree."

Football.title: Man Utd legend's son given six-game ban and fined after homophobic abuse
Football.content:
Man Utd legend's son given six-game ban and fined after homophobic abuse

The incident occurred during Manchester United Under-21s' 5–2 EFL Trophy defeat at Barnsley in October.

View 2 Images Jack Fletcher has been charged by the Football Association for a breach of its rules

Manchester United academy player Jack Fletcher has received a six-match ban by the Football Association after admitting to homophobic abuse. Fletcher, the son of Manchester United legend Darren, has been banned and fined after breaching FA rules 'involving a reference to sexual orientation.'

‌



A statement from the Football Association reads: "INCIDENT OF MISCONDUCT – NON-STANDARD CASE: Jack Fletcher, Player, Manchester United FC. Barnsley FC vs Manchester United FC Reserves - EFL Trophy - Tuesday 21st October 2025

‌



"Breach of FA Rule E3. – It is alleged that during the fixture, you acted in an improper manner and/or used abusive and/or insulting words, contrary to FA Rule E3.1.

‌



"It is alleged that the breach of FA Rule E3.1 is an “Aggravated Breach”, as defined in FA Rule E3.2, as it included a reference, whether express or implied, to sexual orientation.

Why Michael Carrick rejected Newcastle as Man Utd boss's reason for quitting resurfaces READ MORE :

"Jack Fletcher admitted the charge and elected a paper hearing. Decision: six-match suspension, £1,500 fine, Education order."

The 18-year-old, who admitted the charge, was fined £1,500 and was banned for two games for the red card at the time. He has been handed an additional four-game ban after accepting an aggravated breach of FA rule E3.1. He has served three of those four games, with only non-first-team competitive fixtures counting.

‌



"I am truly sorry for the offensive word that I used in the heat of the moment," he said.

"Despite the fact that I had no intention to use the term as a homophobic insult, I completely understand that such language is unacceptable and immediately apologised after the game.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"I want to be clear that this momentary lapse of character absolutely does not reflect my beliefs or values."

A statement released by United in response to the incident read: "Manchester United has worked with Jack to strengthen his understanding of discriminatory language, and why it is harmful.

View 2 Images Jack Fletcher has been in Man United's academy since 2023

Article continues below

"In addition to his ongoing participation in regular Academy programmes on diversity and inclusion, Jack will also take part in educational training through the FA.

"Manchester United prides itself on being an inclusive and welcoming club."

Jack played against Barnsley and even found the net after just seven minutes. He and his twin brother Tyler both feature in the Red Devils' academy.

Football.title: Michael Carrick's ex-Man Utd teammate explains why Red Devils must appoint him full-time
Football.content:
Michael Carrick's ex-Man Utd teammate explains why Red Devils must appoint him full-time

Wes Brown has hailed the impact of Michael Carrick as Manchester United's interim boss - and reckons he is in pole position to get a permanent deal

View 2 Images Michael Carrick has been a revelation since taking charge of Manchester United (Image: Carl Recine/Getty Images)

Wes Brown reckons Michael Carrick is leaving Manchester United with no choice but to make him the club's next permanent manager.

‌



Carrick has been a revelation since taking charge on an interim basis, leading United on an unbeaten run of six wins from seven games.

‌



United have climbed to third in the table and are in pole position to secure a return to the Champions League. And former United star Brown reckons Carrick has done more than enough to get the job on a full-time basis.

‌



Brown told BetMGM : "I think if something is working then why change it? You can't say if Michael will get the job permanently now, but there’s no doubt he’s in contention for it.

"He's seriously in the conversation now after what he’s done since he’s come in.

Two factors that will decide Bruno Fernandes' Man Utd future as skipper ponders next move READ MORE :

Man Utd star Senne Lammens reveals secret to success under Michael Carrick READ MORE :

"It’s a seriously hard job. You just have to look at the managers that have come in before him and how much they've struggled for evidence of that.

‌



"He’s managed to get the players playing well again, and in doing so they’ve won matches, and, at the end of the day, that’s what the Premier League is about.

"In the past people might have had questions about whether he's a big enough name for the job. But I don't think they can really do that now.

View 2 Images Michael Carrick has transformed Manchester United's results (Image: Marc Atkins/Getty Images)

‌



"Manchester United have gone for big names before and they've failed, so I think the fact Michael understands the club and knows what's expected of the players make him a very strong candidate.

"He knows that more than pretty much any manager they could go for - and that's got to count in his favour.

"He's got that experience under his belt now as well, and there's always a case that if they do go down another path and it goes wrong, they'll look back and realise they made the wrong decision.

Article continues below

"Don’t get me wrong, he still hasn’t been there for very long but I don’t think there are many managers that could have done any better than Michael has done."

Football.title: Edwards back at fly-half as Wales make three changes for Ireland
Football.content:
Wales have made three changes for Friday's game with Ireland in Dublin as they bid to end their 14-game losing streak in the Six Nations.

Fly-half Dan Edwards, who started the opening two games against England and France, comes in for the injured Sam Costelow.

Back-rower James Botham replaces the injured Taine Plumtree to make his first start in this season's tournament, with Alex Mann switching to blindside flanker to accommodate his Cardiff colleague, while Ellis Mee comes onto the wing for Gabriel Hamer-Webb, who made his Test debut in the narrow loss to Scotland.

Image: Wales suffered late heartbreak against Scotland in their last match

Head coach Steve Tandy said: "There are a couple of enforced changes this week due to injury and then Ellis had played well in the opening couple of rounds, so we feel he deserves the opportunity again.

"This week it's about backing up the performance against Scotland. Going away from home is a different type of environment for our group, but the focus for us is really improving on what we did last game.

"I thought the defence against Scotland was much improved so ⁠we definitely want to double down on that ⁠going to Ireland.

"You see how they keep the ball in possession, they play lots of passes so we know we're going to ‌have to build and improve from the other week."

Scrum-half Tomos Williams will be alongside Edwards, with Joe Hawkins and Eddie James the centre pairing, while Josh Adams, Mee and full-back Louis Rees-Zammit make ‌up the back three.

Aaron Wainwright has been passed fit to play at No 8 with Botham and Alex Mann the loose trio at the back of the scrum.

Dafydd Jenkins and Ben Carter are the lock pairing, and hooker Dewi Lake, who also captains the side, has props Rhys Carre and ⁠Tomas Francis either side of him.

Exciting young Welsh talent potential debut

Image: Louie Hennessey has been an exciting young talent for Bath and Wales U20s

Tandy could also hand a debut to 21-year-old Louie Hennessey, who plays for Bath, off the bench after he was named in the match-day 23.

"Louie was unfortunate not to get his opportunity in the autumn, but he's trained really well," Tandy said.

"He had a bit of a bump on his hand ⁠at the start of this campaign, but the way he's recovered and put in performances in the training environment has been very impressive."

Ireland have dominated recent meetings with Wales, winning eight of the last ⁠nine matches, with Wales having not won in Dublin since 2015.

Welsh team to face Ireland

Starting XV: 15 Louis Rees-Zammit, 14 Ellis Mee, 13 Eddie James, 12 Joe Hawkins, 11 Josh Adams, 10 Dan Edwards, 9 Tomos Williams; 1 Rhys Carre, 2 Dewi Lake (c), 3 Tomas Francis, 4 Dafydd Jenkins, 5 Ben Carter, 6 Alex Mann, 7 James Botham, 8 Aaron Wainwright.

Replacements: 16 Ryan Elias, 17 Nicky Smith, 18 Archie Griffin, 19 Adam ‌Beard, 20 Olly Cracknell, 21 Kieran Hardy, 22 Jarrod Evans, 23 Louie Hennessey.

Football.title: Gasly: F1 will make 'right decision' on Middle East races amid conflict
Football.content:
Alpine driver Pierre Gasly says Formula 1 will make the "right decision" on whether to go ahead with the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grand Prix in April.

Both races are in doubt amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East after US and Israeli strikes on Iran, which saw Iran launch attacks on other countries in the Gulf, including Bahrain and Saudi Arabia - two countries which have US bases.

The opening three F1 races, starting with this weekend's Australian Grand Prix, followed by races in China and Japan later this month, will not be impacted.

But, F1 are "closely monitoring" the Middle East conflict before making a final decision on the scheduled rounds in Bahrain on April 10-12 and Saudi Arabia on April 17-19.

Alpine's Gasly told Sky Sports News: "I trust the people in charge. There's so much at stake.

"I have some family living in the Middle East, and people close to me. It's difficult to get my head around everything that's going on when you know what's happening at the moment.

"Hopefully things will get better, but, ultimately, F1 will make the right decision and what's best for all of us."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports F1's David Croft takes a closer look at how F1 in 2026 will evolve as the sport begins a new era of recharge, featuring more electric power, new teams, and engine suppliers

On Tuesday, the World Endurance Championship announced their season-opener in Qatar on March 26-28 had been postponed.

In its WEC statement, FIA president Mohammed Ben Sulayem said: "The FIA continues to monitor developments across the region and our thoughts remain with all those affected by these recent events as we hope for calm, safety and a return to stability."

Formula 1 2026 Calendar Australia March 6-8 Hungary July 24-26 China March 13-15* Netherlands August 21-23* Japan March 27-29 Italy September 4-6 Bahrain April 10-12 Madrid September 11-13 Saudi Arabia April 17-19 Azerbaijan September 24-26 Miami May 1-3* Singapore October 9-11* Canada May 22-24* USA October 23-25 Monaco June 5-7 Mexico Oct 30-Nov 1 Barcelona June 12-14 Brazil November 6-8 Austria June 26-28 Las Vegas November 19-21 Great Britain July 3-5* Qatar November 27-29 Belgium July 17-19 Abu Dhabi December 4-6 *denotes Sprint weekend

What would happen if Bahrain and Saudi Arabia F1 races are cancelled?

Sky Sports' Nigel Chiu:

"Formula 1 will first hope the Middle East conflict ends in the coming days, but if it does not, they will no doubt be making contingency plans.

"From a calendar perspective, there is a three-week gap in May and the traditional four-week break in August where the teams' factories must be shut down for at least two of those weeks.

"Aside from this, every other round is either held straight after another race or just two weeks following the previous event. That means moving even just one of the Bahrain or Saudi Arabia races would create a triple-header, or even four successive race weekends.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Oscar Piastri looks ahead to the new F1 season and the challenges McLaren will face with the new regulations

"Portimao in Portugal and Imola in Italy would be likely candidates to replace Bahrain and/or Saudi Arabia if the Middle East events are cancelled completely. If F1 want any chance of staging those races in April though, they must make a decision by mid-March at the latest.

"Should there be no races in April or the three-week gap in May, only the Miami Grand Prix (May 1-3) would be staged following the Japanese Grand Prix at the end of March to the Canadian Grand Prix towards the end of May."

Football.title: 'You definitely question yourself': Draper confident after tough recovery
Football.content:
Jack Draper revealed he had started to question himself during the injury setback that derailed the end of his 2025 season, but is confident he is ready both physically and mentally for his comeback.

Having won in Indian Wells in 2025, his first ATP Masters 1000 title, Draper withdrew from the US Open in September with an arm injury, before returning in last week's Dubai Tennis Championship.

Draper suffered a second-round defeat to Arthur Rinderknech in Dubai and, ahead of his title defence in California, live on Sky Sports this week, the British No 1 says he is in a "good place" after a gruelling lay-off.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Draper made his official return to the Tour at the Dubai Tennis Championships with a straight sets win over Quentin Halys in February

"This injury has definitely hardened me a fair bit," Draper said.

"I've had certain injuries in the past but when you do so well and when you get to the goal that you finally want to get to, being in a good position, competing for great tournaments and then something like this happens - you definitely start to question yourself and it makes you quite sad in a way.

"But it's always about how you pick yourself up.

"I always think there's only one response to any sort of hardship or adversity and that's to improve and be better for it.

"So, I'm proud that I'm coming here in a good place. Mentally, I feel good. Physically, I'm in good shape.

"I wouldn't be back if I wasn't, I'm just so happy to be here and hopefully I can have a great week."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of Draper's win over Carlos Alcaraz in the Indian Wells semi-final in 2025

On his way to winning Indian Wells last year, Draper defeated Carlos Alcaraz, the world No 1 and now seven-time Grand Slam Champion, in the semi-final after losing the second set 6-0, before beating Denmark's Holger Rune in the final.

As a seeded player, Draper will begin in round two, while the first round begins on Wednesday, with coverage starting at 7pm.

Watch the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells live on Sky Sports from March 4-15. Stream tennis with NOW, contract-free.

Football.title: Azure Angel and Equality clash at Southwell
Football.content:
We have a busy day’s racing on Wednesday with all-weather action from Southwell, live on Sky Sports Racing...

4.02 Southwell - Azure Angel and Equality clash

An excellent Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Handicap sees Azure Angel and Equality clash over five furlongs.

Azure Angel has a fine strike-rate on the all-weather, winning five of her 10 starts including when staying on strongly to score over this course and distance in September. She is back from a 164-day layoff with the blinkers retained and must have claims despite a career-high mark.

Charlie Hills' Equality has dropped to a workable mark having failed to get his head in front in the last few seasons. He was not beaten far when fourth at Newcastle in January and may benefit from the dip in class.

Gaeli has plenty of useful form in Italy and enters calculations on debut for Marco Botti and Jack Gilligan, while last-time winner Spring Is Sprung is another for the shortlist.

4.32 Southwell - Well-bred That's Amore returns for Beckett

A fascinating Make The Move To Midnite Novice Stakes where That's Amore headlines a field of 11.

The Ralph Beckett-trained three-year-old has a lovely pedigree, being a sister to Group Three winner Persica, and she made a stunning start to her career when winning by six lengths at Newbury in October. Sent off just 9-4 when fourth behind Minnie Hauk in the Cheshire Oaks next time, she has not been seen since and rates a filly to watch with market clues eagerly anticipated.

Jupiter Ammon, rated 82 after eight starts without success, has had a gelding operation since finishing a solid fifth in a Class Two handicap at York and looks the most likely to capitalise if the favourite disappoints.

Grazeon Sunshine is a national hunt flat winner and should strip fitter for his run behind Constitution Hill here.

5.40 Southwell - Tarbat Ness seeks a four-timer

Prolific Tarbat Ness bids for more glory in this Midnite Aint Your Grandads Bookie Handicap at 5.40pm.

John Berry's charge has been in cracking form in the past couple of months and romped clear of his rivals at Newcastle 17 days ago. Given the way he settled matters, he ought to prove tough to beat under this seven-pound penalty with the extra couple of furlongs holding few fears.

Hatysa is a progressive stayer for the Andrew Balding team, winning three times since July. She ran a solid third after a break at Newcastle last month and rates a huge danger dropped into this 0-80.

Barenboim narrowly failed to make all when second at Lingfield on his last start and must be considered, while Art Zoosve hopes to bounce back from a lesser run last time.
